reasoning: The informal statement asserts that any subgroup H of G is generated by H \ {1}. The Lean statement encodes this directly: `Subgroup.closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = H`, where `(H : Set G)` is the underlying set of H, `\ {1}` removes the identity, and `Subgroup.closure` takes the subgroup generated by this set. The equality to H expresses "H is generated by H - {1}". The hypotheses (G is a group, H is a subgroup) match the informal setup. No extra assumptions are introduced and no assumptions are omitted, so this is a faithful formalization.
